name: Firecrawl
notes: Self-serve signup gives an fc- API key (bearer) with a free tier against a public REST API, so building is easy.

name: Clay
notes: Clay has no self-serve query API; only per-table webhooks/HTTP actions plus an Enterprise-only People & Company lookup API, so real data retrieval is gated to paid Enterprise.

name: GitHub
notes: Anyone can self-serve a fine-grained/classic PAT (or OAuth/GitHub App JWT) against the public REST (and GraphQL) API, making it easy to build.

name: Snowflake
notes: SQL REST API uses key-pair JWT or OAuth and there is no permanent free tier (30-day/$400 trial only), so key-pair JWT setup plus trial gating make it moderate.

name: Notion
notes: Self-serve internal integration token (bearer) or OAuth2 against a free public REST API makes building easy.
